sets.methodology: BuildKit Draft Score = 0.75 * z(shrink(GIH WR, k=200)) + 0.25 * z(shrink(IWD, k=200)), computed from 17lands' public PremierDraft card_ratings (CC BY 4.0). shrink(x, n, k=200) = (n*x + k*mean(x over the pool)) / (n + k) is empirical-Bayes shrinkage toward the pool mean weighted by n = GIH game_count, so two cards with the SAME raw win rate but different sample sizes never tie: the low-sample card regresses harder toward the mean and cannot outrank the high-sample card. z(y) = (y - mean(y)) / population_stdev(y) is computed across EVERY card in the pool regardless of rarity -- rarity-neutral, no separate curve for commons vs mythics. Grade bands on the final score: S>=+1.25, A>=+0.5, B>=-0.25, C>=-1.0, D>=-2.0, else F; a card with no GIH data at all is 'unrated'. GIH WR = ever_drawn_win_rate, IWD = drawn_improvement_win_rate, ALSA = avg_seen, ATA = avg_pick, OH WR = opening_hand_win_rate -- 17lands' own field names, carried through unchanged. Per-color-pair rows use the identical formula computed over just that pair's card pool; a pair only publishes when 17lands' response for it is verifiably distinct from the set's overall response (seventeenlands.pair_data_is_distinct) -- 17lands' public no-auth endpoint does not currently filter by color pair for every set/pair combination, and a non-distinct pair is omitted rather than republished as fake per-pair signal. Every card in this set's own card list (Scryfall's set-tagged gameplay cards, UNIONED with every name 17lands itself tracks for this set -- some sets carry 'bonus sheet' cards whose own Scryfall printing carries a different set code) gets a real Draft Score grade here -- never 'unrated' and never silently omitted (Kruz's no-bare-unrated rule, extended from the Planar Cube module to every standard premier set). Each card is graded by the BEST available signal, tried in this order, with the basis always shown on the row: (1) this set's own live 17lands GIH win rate, when its sample clears 100 ever-drawn games ('17lands live data'); (2) that SAME live win rate below that floor -- still real data, but shrunk MUCH harder toward the set's own pool mean (k=3000 vs the normal k=200) and always rendered at 'sample' confidence ('17lands live data (thin sample)'); (3) a cross-set prior -- this set's own currently-active premier-draft data (reused from the Draft Ranker's own fetch) plus 17lands' public bulk S3 game-data archive (CC BY 4.0, the SAME machinery the Planar Cube module uses) for a real historical GIH win rate from whichever of the 32 archived premier sets the card was last actually drafted in -- resolved via the card's own newest printing when that already IS an archived set, else a live Scryfall prints search (api.scryfall.com/cards/search, cached 30 days) for an older printing that is; (4) a HEURISTIC prior when none of the above have data -- the IDENTICAL transparent rarity/mana-value/type-line/edhrec_rank score the Planar Cube module uses (see CUBE_HEURISTIC_* constants), never a win-rate proxy. Confidence on a (3) cross-set-prior row is capped at 'low' no matter how large the borrowed sample was -- a card's performance in a DIFFERENT set is real data but not the same signal as this set's own draft environment. Heuristic (4) rows always carry 'sample' confidence, the lowest tier, rendered faded. pair_rows and sealed_rows are NOT covered by this gap-fill chain -- they keep the prior floor-gated behavior (a card below 17lands' sample floor there still renders 'unrated'). EARLY DATA -- upgrading as samples grow (150,696 total games so far). 17lands' color-pair filter returned no data distinct from the overall set this run, so pair_rows is empty rather than a duplicate of overall_rows.

sets.sealed_methodology: BuildKit Draft Score = 0.75 * z(shrink(GIH WR, k=200)) + 0.25 * z(shrink(IWD, k=200)), computed from 17lands' public PremierDraft card_ratings (CC BY 4.0). shrink(x, n, k=200) = (n*x + k*mean(x over the pool)) / (n + k) is empirical-Bayes shrinkage toward the pool mean weighted by n = GIH game_count, so two cards with the SAME raw win rate but different sample sizes never tie: the low-sample card regresses harder toward the mean and cannot outrank the high-sample card. z(y) = (y - mean(y)) / population_stdev(y) is computed across EVERY card in the pool regardless of rarity -- rarity-neutral, no separate curve for commons vs mythics. Grade bands on the final score: S>=+1.25, A>=+0.5, B>=-0.25, C>=-1.0, D>=-2.0, else F; a card with no GIH data at all is 'unrated'. GIH WR = ever_drawn_win_rate, IWD = drawn_improvement_win_rate, ALSA = avg_seen, ATA = avg_pick, OH WR = opening_hand_win_rate -- 17lands' own field names, carried through unchanged. Per-color-pair rows use the identical formula computed over just that pair's card pool; a pair only publishes when 17lands' response for it is verifiably distinct from the set's overall response (seventeenlands.pair_data_is_distinct) -- 17lands' public no-auth endpoint does not currently filter by color pair for every set/pair combination, and a non-distinct pair is omitted rather than republished as fake per-pair signal. Sealed rows use the IDENTICAL BuildKit Draft Score formula computed fresh over the Sealed card pool's own mean/stdev (a card's sealed grade is never mixed with its draft grade) -- only the sample-confidence bins differ (Sealed: (20, 80, 300), vs draft's (100, 400, 1500)) since Sealed's per-card game counts run structurally lower. Sourced from 17lands' public card_ratings endpoint with `format=Sealed` (CC BY 4.0). 17lands' public Sealed card_ratings endpoint returned 8,506 ever-drawn games but NO per-card win-rate stats this run (verified live 2026-07-11: win rates appear unavailable for Sealed via this public no-auth route even when game-count totals are non-zero) -- sealed_rows ships honest-absent rather than a page of every card graded 'unrated'.

sets.methodology: BuildKit Draft Score = 0.75 * z(shrink(GIH WR, k=200)) + 0.25 * z(shrink(IWD, k=200)), computed from 17lands' public PremierDraft card_ratings (CC BY 4.0). shrink(x, n, k=200) = (n*x + k*mean(x over the pool)) / (n + k) is empirical-Bayes shrinkage toward the pool mean weighted by n = GIH game_count, so two cards with the SAME raw win rate but different sample sizes never tie: the low-sample card regresses harder toward the mean and cannot outrank the high-sample card. z(y) = (y - mean(y)) / population_stdev(y) is computed across EVERY card in the pool regardless of rarity -- rarity-neutral, no separate curve for commons vs mythics. Grade bands on the final score: S>=+1.25, A>=+0.5, B>=-0.25, C>=-1.0, D>=-2.0, else F; a card with no GIH data at all is 'unrated'. GIH WR = ever_drawn_win_rate, IWD = drawn_improvement_win_rate, ALSA = avg_seen, ATA = avg_pick, OH WR = opening_hand_win_rate -- 17lands' own field names, carried through unchanged. Per-color-pair rows use the identical formula computed over just that pair's card pool; a pair only publishes when 17lands' response for it is verifiably distinct from the set's overall response (seventeenlands.pair_data_is_distinct) -- 17lands' public no-auth endpoint does not currently filter by color pair for every set/pair combination, and a non-distinct pair is omitted rather than republished as fake per-pair signal. This set's 17lands card_ratings response totalled 97 ever-drawn games this run -- below the 5,000-game publish floor, so no rows are graded.

cube.methodology: IMPORTANT: these are DAY-1 PRIORS, NOT real Planar Cube win rates -- Planar Cube Draft launched on MTG Arena 2026-07-21 and 17lands has not yet accumulated per-card telemetry for it (verified live 2026-07-21: their public 'Cube - Planar' card_ratings endpoint returns HTTP 200 with zero rows). EVERY card in the pool (the constant 200-card planar core + the current week's 360-card rotating planar module, per Wizards' own announcement article) gets a real tier -- we never withhold a grade behind an 'unrated' placeholder. Each card is graded by the BEST available signal, tried in this order, with the basis always shown on the row: (1) REAL live 'Cube - Planar' data, the instant 17lands starts publishing a real win rate for that card -- this module upgrades automatically, since the live fetch is attempted every refresh; (2) a 'Cube - Powered' prior (the prior Arena Powered Cube event, June 2-23 2026) -- REAL historical per-card win rates we aggregate ourselves from 17lands' public bulk S3 game-data archive (one CSV row per completed game, CC BY 4.0, whitelisted in METAHUB-SPEC.md's legal rails), since that event's LIVE endpoint only ever returns near-empty totals for an ended/rotated-out queue; (3) a cross-set prior -- that SAME bulk-archive mechanism applied to each card's own designated historical premier set (its Scryfall oracle-cards representative printing's set, already-cached bulk data, cross-checked against 17lands' real bulk-dataset catalog), so a 20-year-old reprint gets its OWN most recent premier-draft set's real GIH (games-in-hand) win rate rather than a guess -- current-set live data (today's active premier set, reused from the Draft Ranker's own fetch) is tried first per card, the bulk archive fills every set the live queue can't reach; sets covering fewer than a handful of pool cards are skipped (logged, never silently) since the point of a multi-ten-MB archive download isn't worth it for one card -- those cards fall to (4) same as any other real 'no prior data' case; (4) a HEURISTIC prior when none of the above have data -- a transparent, documented (NOT statistical) score from the card's own Scryfall-grounded rarity + mana value + type line PLUS a bucketed `edhrec_rank` popularity bonus (also already in the cached Scryfall bulk -- grounded, no EDHREC scraping; NEVER a win-rate proxy, just more transparent points on the same never-comparable scale -- see CUBE_HEURISTIC_* constants for the exact weights), clearly labeled 'heuristic' and never presented as a win rate or a real performance signal. BuildKit Draft Score (the identical empirical-Bayes-shrunk z-score formula as the Draft Ranker -- see DRAFT_SCORE_METHODOLOGY, k=200 games) is computed over ONLY the cube pool's own mean/stdev of whatever REAL priors ((1)/(2)/(3)) were found this run -- a prior card's score is never benchmarked against its original set's baseline, and the empirical-Bayes shrinkage means a thin-sample cross-set card (a few dozen bulk-archive games) and a thick-sample one (thousands) never read the same just because their raw win rates happen to match; heuristic rows never get a draft_score (they carry heuristic_score instead, on its own separate never-comparable scale). Confidence on any (2)/(3) prior row is capped at 'low' no matter how large the borrowed sample was -- a card's performance in a DIFFERENT format/cube/set is real data but not the same signal as its performance in Planar Cube, so it can never earn 'medium'/'high' from a borrowed prior. Heuristic (4) rows always carry 'sample' confidence -- the lowest tier, rendered faded -- since no performance data backs them at all. Only a real (1) live-data row can reach 'medium'/'high' confidence. If very few cards found a real prior this run, the z-score itself can be mathematically undefined (a population of one has no variance) -- those rows fall back to a plain win-rate-floor grade (S>=56%, A>=53%, B>=50.5%, C>=48%, else D -- the same floors as the Limited tier list) so a real recorded win rate is never hidden behind an 'unrated' placeholder either. Uncertainty is communicated ONLY through confidence + fading + the visible basis label -- never by omitting a tier.

hob.methodology: SPOILER SEASON -- these are DAY-0 PRIORS, NOT real Hobbit draft win rates. The Hobbit (HOB) launches on MTG Arena 2026-08-11; no game has been played with these cards yet, so 17lands cannot have ANY per-card telemetry for this set (a harder gap than the Planar Cube module's Day-1 state, which at least had a live endpoint that COULD have started reporting data -- HOB genuinely has zero chance of that until launch). Every REVEALED card (per Wizards' officially announced 188-card main set; revealed_count/total_card_count/spoiler_progress on this module track how much of the set is public) still gets a real tier -- never 'unrated' -- from the SAME fallback chain the standard-set Draft Ranker gap-fill uses below its own live/thin-sample tiers: (1) a cross-set prior -- a real historical GIH win rate for a REPRINTED card, from any currently-active premier set's live data or 17lands' public bulk S3 archive (CC BY 4.0), resolved via a live Scryfall prints search for genuine reprints; (2) a HEURISTIC prior for a brand-new card -- the IDENTICAL transparent rarity/mana-value/type-line/edhrec_rank score the Planar Cube and standard-set gap-fill modules use, never a win-rate proxy. Confidence on a cross-set-prior row is capped at 'low'; heuristic rows always carry 'sample' confidence, the lowest tier, rendered faded. Card data is Scryfall's own -- the cached bulk oracle-cards dump UNIONED with a live per-set search (api.scryfall.com/cards/search) to catch spoilers the cached dump hasn't picked up yet, both the SAME whitelisted Scryfall source METAHUB-SPEC.md already covers. On 2026-08-11 HOB flows into the STANDARD Draft Ranker pipeline like any other premier set (no special-casing needed, per the 2026-07-21 STATUS.md note) and this spoiler-season module is superseded by real 17lands telemetry.
